impact_pathways.expected_impact_description: Contribution to and implementation of the European Apply AI Strategy, advancing the adoption of next-generation AI agent capabilities in key economic sectors including healthcare, advanced manufacturing, and in-vehicle autonomous driving.
impact_pathways.impact_narrative: MAESTRO's clinical decision support agent system (OUT-4, D5-02, WP5) and manufacturing process optimisation agent system (OUT-5, D6-02, WP6) directly advance the European Apply AI Strategy by deploying next-generation multi-agent AI in two of the three priority sectors identified in the call. D5-02 provides validated diagnostic reasoning evidence on 500 de-identified clinical cases, with auditable reasoning traces conforming to EU AI Act high-risk system transparency requirements, establishing a validated pathway for healthcare sector adoption. D6-02 demonstrates measurable quality and efficiency improvements — ≥15% defect reduction and ≥10% energy savings — in a pilot factory environment, constituting direct evidence of AI agent readiness for advanced manufacturing deployment in line with Apply AI Strategy objectives.

impact_pathways.expected_impact_description: Wider deployment and industrial uptake of next-generation AI agent solutions across economic sectors via European Digital Innovation Hubs (EDIHs), enabling broader societal and economic benefit.
impact_pathways.impact_narrative: EDIH-mediated wider deployment is operationalised through three channels explicitly linked to project deliverables: (1) the healthcare demonstrator evaluation dataset (D5-02, WP5) constitutes the clinical evidence base for EDIH-mediated dissemination to ≥3 hospital networks across Europe by M48, as stated in IMP-2 indicators; (2) the manufacturing process optimisation demonstrator software (D6-02, WP6) is scaled through ≥2 EDIHs and the EIT Manufacturing network, targeting ≥5 manufacturing SMEs in technology transfer by M48, as stated in IMP-3 indicators; (3) the open-source MAESTRO framework (D9-02, WP9) and benchmark suite (D8-02, WP8) are actively disseminated through ≥3 EDIHs to European AI developers and SMEs, lowering barriers to AI agent adoption across economic sectors, as stated in IMP-5 indicators. All three channels are grounded in explicit Tier 3 linkage data, not generic EDIH language.

impact_pathways.expected_impact_description: Open scientific advancement: dissemination and sharing of AI agent R&D results with the European research community through the AI-on-demand platform, strengthening European AI research infrastructure.

outcomes.outcome_title: Neuro-symbolic planning framework for LLM-based agents
Formally verified multi-agent coordination protocol suite
External Tool and API Orchestration Layer (TRL 5)

impact_pathways.impact_narrative: Open scientific advancement and AI-on-demand platform contribution operate through two explicitly linked mechanisms: (1) ≥20 peer-reviewed publications reporting results from the neuro-symbolic planning engine (OUT-1, D2-02, WP2), adaptive tri-store memory system (OUT-2, D3-02, WP3), and formally verified coordination software (OUT-3, D4-02, WP4) disseminate MAESTRO's core R&D to the European AI research community, with a target of ≥100 citations per publication within 3 years; (2) the open-source MAESTRO framework (D9-02, WP9), MAESTRO benchmark suite (D8-02, WP8), and evaluation framework specification (D8-01, WP8) are contributed to the AI-on-demand platform, directly strengthening European AI research infrastructure by providing reusable, openly accessible agent components and standardised evaluation protocols. These deliverables enable European researchers and SMEs to build on MAESTRO advances without creating dependency on proprietary non-European platforms.

impact_pathways.expected_impact_description: Strengthening European open strategic autonomy in digital and emerging enabling technologies through advances in next-generation AI agent capabilities.
impact_pathways.traceability_note: EI-04 is classified Inferred in expected_impacts.json (inference from topic destination placement). Coverage is provided based on that documented inference; no explicit expected_impact statement exists in the call scope or expected_outcome sections for this impact.

outcomes.outcome_title: Neuro-symbolic planning framework for LLM-based agents
Formally verified multi-agent coordination protocol suite
External Tool and API Orchestration Layer (TRL 5)

impact_pathways.impact_narrative: European open strategic autonomy in next-generation AI agents is strengthened through two project-specific mechanisms with explicit Tier 3 linkage: (1) the neuro-symbolic planning engine (OUT-1, D2-02, WP2), adaptive tri-store memory system (OUT-2, D3-02, WP3), and decentralised coordination software (OUT-3, D4-02, WP4) collectively establish a European-origin technical paradigm for trustworthy AI agents, architecturally independent of US and Chinese proprietary platforms — IMP-1 explicitly characterises this as differentiating from non-European scale-only approaches; (2) the formally verified multi-agent coordination protocol specification (D4-01, WP4) is submitted to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE, positioning Europe at the forefront of international multi-agent AI standardisation aligned with EU AI Act transparency requirements, as stated in IMP-6 indicators. The open-source MAESTRO framework (D9-02, WP9) sustains this autonomy post-project by enabling continued European development without proprietary dependencies.

impact_pathways.expected_impact_description: Unlocking the economic potential of AI agents across diverse economic sectors, including data analytics and coding, by advancing agent architecture, memory, reasoning, and autonomous behaviour.

outcomes.outcome_title: Logistics and supply chain transfer demonstrator (TRL 5)

impact_pathways.impact_narrative: The economic potential of AI agents across diverse sectors is unlocked through two explicitly linked project mechanisms: (1) the logistics and supply chain transfer demonstrator (OUT-6, D7-02, WP7) validates MAESTRO architecture portability beyond the call's three primary sectors, demonstrating ≥20% improvement in disruption recovery time across ≥3 international supply chain corridors and producing an architectural transferability mapping to in-vehicle autonomous driving — establishing that MAESTRO's planning, memory, coordination, and tool orchestration stack generates economic value in logistics, automotive, and analogous domains not named in the call scope; (2) the open-source MAESTRO framework (D9-02, WP9) and benchmark suite (D8-02, WP8) lower entry barriers for European SMEs and startups developing AI agent applications in data analytics, coding, and further economic domains, with ≥10 SMEs/startups building on MAESTRO by M48 as stated in IMP-5 indicators. Both mechanisms are grounded in explicit Tier 3 linkage data.

kpis.description: Clinical decision support demonstrator validation: number of de-identified clinical cases processed through the MAESTRO-based diagnostic reasoning system and acceptance of clinical validation report by a national health technology assessment body
kpis.target: 500 de-identified clinical cases processed and validated; clinical validation report accepted by ≥1 national health technology assessment body by M48
kpis.measurement_method: Clinical expert review against diagnostic benchmark designed in WP5 T5-01; health technology assessment body acceptance recorded; evidence captured in healthcare demonstrator evaluation dataset

kpis.description: Manufacturing process optimisation demonstrator: defect rate reduction and energy savings demonstrated in pilot factory environment against pre-deployment baseline
kpis.target: ≥15% defect reduction and ≥10% energy savings versus pre-deployment baseline, demonstrated by M42
kpis.measurement_method: Before/after operational measurement during WP6 T6-03 pilot factory validation; metrics recorded in manufacturing process optimisation demonstrator software outputs

kpis.description: Number of EDIHs actively disseminating MAESTRO technology and number of industrial adopters (hospital networks and manufacturing SMEs) engaged in adoption planning or technology transfer
kpis.target: ≥3 EDIHs actively disseminating MAESTRO technology; ≥3 hospital networks engaged in post-project adoption planning by M48; ≥5 manufacturing SMEs in technology transfer by M48
kpis.measurement_method: EDIH engagement agreements and partner activity reports; hospital network and SME adoption plan documentation; all recorded in final dissemination and impact report

kpis.description: Adoption of MAESTRO benchmark suite by external research groups as evidence of wider AI research ecosystem industrial uptake
kpis.target: Benchmark suite adopted by ≥5 external research groups by M48
kpis.measurement_method: Adoption tracking via citation and reference analysis; external group adoption survey administered through WP9 community channels; uptake documented in benchmark suite dataset record

kpis.description: Number of peer-reviewed publications in top-tier AI venues (NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, AAMAS, AAAI) reporting MAESTRO R&D results, and aggregate citation impact
kpis.target: ≥20 peer-reviewed publications in top-tier AI venues by M60; ≥100 citations per MAESTRO publication within 3 years of publication
kpis.measurement_method: Curated publication list maintained in final dissemination and impact report; citation counts tracked via Google Scholar and Semantic Scholar at 12, 24, and 36 months post-publication

kpis.description: Availability of MAESTRO open-source framework on the AI-on-demand platform and uptake measured by GitHub repository activity following public release
kpis.target: MAESTRO framework and benchmark suite contributed to AI-on-demand platform by M36; ≥500 GitHub stars within 6 months of public release
kpis.measurement_method: AI-on-demand platform registration confirmation received; GitHub repository star, fork, and contributor metrics recorded at 3 and 6 months post-release

kpis.description: Standardisation proposals for MAESTRO multi-agent coordination protocols submitted to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE and external adoption of the protocol specification by research projects
kpis.target: ≥1 standardisation proposal submitted to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE by M48; protocol specification adopted or referenced by ≥3 external multi-agent research projects
kpis.measurement_method: Submission confirmation from the relevant standards body secretariat; adoption and citation tracking for the protocol specification document; documented in multi-agent coordination protocol specification

kpis.description: European scientific recognition of MAESTRO as a European-origin AI agent paradigm, measured by invited keynote presentations at major international AI conferences
kpis.target: ≥3 invited keynotes at major international AI conferences (NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, AAMAS, AAAI, or equivalent) delivered by MAESTRO consortium members by M60
kpis.measurement_method: Conference acceptance records and keynote programme confirmations; compiled and documented in final integration and evaluation report

kpis.description: Logistics transfer demonstrator: disruption recovery time improvement versus baseline and number of international supply chain corridors operationally validated
kpis.target: ≥20% improvement in disruption recovery time versus pre-deployment baseline; operational validation completed across ≥3 international supply chain corridors; architectural transferability mapping to in-vehicle autonomous driving completed by M48
kpis.measurement_method: Operational validation data collected during WP7 T7-03; disruption recovery baseline and post-deployment metrics recorded across corridors; transferability mapping documented in cross-sector generalisation evaluation dataset

kpis.description: European SME and startup adoption of MAESTRO open-source framework for development of AI agent applications in diverse economic sectors including data analytics and coding
kpis.target: ≥10 European SMEs/startups actively building on the MAESTRO framework by M48
kpis.measurement_method: Adoption survey administered through WP9 T9-01 community channels and EDIH partner reports; GitHub repository fork and contributor activity; tracked in open-source software release documentation

dissemination_plan.open_access_policy: All MAESTRO scientific publications are made open-access immediately upon acceptance, deposited in discipline-specific repositories (arXiv for AI and computer science preprints) and institutional open-access archives, in compliance with Horizon Europe open science requirements and the project grant agreement. The MAESTRO open-source software framework (WP9), agent evaluation benchmark suite (WP8), evaluation framework specification (WP8), and multi-agent coordination protocol specification (WP4) are released under the Apache 2.0 open-source licence, enabling unrestricted access, reuse, and derivative development by European researchers, SMEs, and startups without proprietary dependency. Research datasets generated by the project — including the healthcare demonstrator evaluation dataset (D5-02), manufacturing demonstrator operational data (D6-02), cross-sector generalisation evaluation dataset (D7-02), and MAESTRO benchmark suite (D8-02) — are made FAIR-compliant (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable) and deposited in appropriate domain repositories with metadata records registered on the AI-on-demand platform. A Data Management Plan is delivered as a distinct project deliverable within the first 6 months of the grant signature and updated at each reporting period.

activities.activity_type: Healthcare sector technology transfer via EDIH network partnerships
activities.expected_result: ≥3 European hospital networks formally engaged in post-project adoption planning for MAESTRO clinical decision support agent system by M48; clinical validation report accepted by ≥1 national health technology assessment body; auditable EU AI Act-compliant reasoning traces establish regulatory pathway for healthcare AI agent deployment

activities.activity_type: Manufacturing sector technology transfer via EIT Manufacturing network and EDIH partnerships
activities.expected_result: ≥5 European manufacturing SMEs in active technology transfer for MAESTRO manufacturing process optimisation multi-agent system by M48; at least ≥2 EDIH formal partnership agreements for industrial scaling; ≥15% defect reduction and ≥10% energy savings demonstrated in pilot factory as evidence base for commercial exploitation

activities.activity_type: Open-source ecosystem commercialisation enabling SME and startup product development
activities.expected_result: ≥10 European SMEs and startups actively building AI agent products and services on the MAESTRO open-source framework by M48; ≥500 GitHub repository stars within 6 months of public release at M36; European AI agent ecosystem reduced dependency on non-European proprietary platforms

activities.activity_type: Multi-agent coordination protocol standardisation and external project adoption
activities.expected_result: ≥1 standardisation proposal submitted to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE by M48; formally verified multi-agent coordination protocol specification adopted or referenced by ≥3 external multi-agent AI research projects; EU AI Act alignment report for multi-agent transparency requirements published

activities.activity_type: Logistics sector operational deployment and ELI integration roadmap
activities.expected_result: MAESTRO multi-agent planning and coordination stack operationally validated across ≥3 international supply chain corridors demonstrating ≥20% improvement in disruption recovery time; integration roadmap for ELI operational systems finalised by M48; architectural transferability mapping to in-vehicle autonomous driving completed and disseminated

exploitation_plan.ipr_strategy: MAESTRO adopts a differentiated intellectual property strategy aligned with the project's dual commitments to open science and commercial exploitation. Core platform components — the MAESTRO open-source framework, agent evaluation benchmark suite, evaluation framework specification, and multi-agent coordination protocol specification — are released under the Apache 2.0 licence to maximise European researcher and SME adoption and ensure compliance with Horizon Europe open access requirements. Sector-specific algorithmic innovations with direct commercial application potential, developed within WP5 (clinical decision support) and WP6 (manufacturing process optimisation), are assessed for patent or trade secret protection by the responsible consortium partners in accordance with the consortium agreement IP provisions prior to publication. The formally verified coordination protocols (WP4) are submitted to standardisation bodies on a royalty-free basis to ensure universal accessibility to European organisations. Background IP contributed by each consortium partner remains the exclusive property of that partner; foreground IP arising jointly from collaborative work packages is governed by joint ownership provisions in the consortium agreement, with licensing terms defined to prioritise European exploitation.

sustainability_mechanism.description: MAESTRO results are sustained beyond the 60-month project duration through three reinforcing mechanisms, each grounded in explicit project impact data from impacts.json. First, the open-source MAESTRO framework (WP9, IMP-5) is maintained as a living codebase under the Apache 2.0 licence on GitHub and registered on the AI-on-demand platform, enabling continued community-driven development without dependency on project funding; the target of ≥10 European SMEs and startups actively building on the framework by M48 creates a self-sustaining developer and user ecosystem with independent motivation for continued maintenance and enhancement. Second, operational deployments in healthcare and manufacturing sectors are institutionalised through EDIH and EIT Manufacturing structural partnerships established during M36–M60: ≥3 hospital networks engaged in adoption planning for the clinical decision support agent system (IMP-2) and ≥5 manufacturing SMEs in technology transfer for the manufacturing optimisation system (IMP-3) create post-project demand-pull and sector-level maintenance obligations undertaken by the respective industrial partners. Third, the formally verified multi-agent coordination protocols (WP4, IMP-6) are embedded in international standards processes through ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE submission by M48, ensuring that MAESTRO's foundational coordination innovations persist within the regulatory and technical standards landscape independently of consortium activity. The MAESTRO benchmark suite (WP8, IMP-5), adopted by ≥5 external research groups by M48, further sustains the project's evaluation infrastructure through community curation.
